title: Helix Investor Pitch Research Report
description: Walkthrough of the comprehensive research report produced for the Helix for ERPs investor pitch deck and gethelix.ai website update

steps.title: Report Header & Table of Contents
steps.description: The report opens with a confidentiality notice and table of contents linking to all 9 sections plus appendix. Designed for dual use: pitch deck prompt context and website content source.

steps.title: Section 1: Executive Narrative
steps.description: The narrative spine anchored by the Manifesto thesis: 'Intelligence is not the product. Responsibility is.' Covers the problem ($2B+ consultant spend), the vision (ERPs becoming databases), the elevator pitch, and the 'why now' argument.

steps.title: Section 2: Product Capabilities
steps.description: Evidence-backed feature inventory with 16 capabilities, each citing specific codebase files. The 9-step autonomous pipeline is the centerpiece, with key differentiators (database branching, deployment automation, audit trails) highlighted for investors.

steps.title: Section 3: Market Sizing (TAM/SAM/SOM)
steps.description: Layered market sizing with external validation: TAM ($5.82B from Precedence Research), SAM ($1.5B-$8.3B from 3 independent sources), SOM ($90M-$180M realistic near-term). The $2B+ claim is positioned as SAM with qualifiers.

steps.title: Section 4: Financial Model Analysis
steps.description: Dovie projections reproduced with 3-scenario stress test. Base case: profitable month 6, $18M ARR at month 36. Conservative case still profitable by month 14. Assumption callout box transparently flags flat-cost and zero-churn assumptions.

steps.title: Section 5: Competitive Landscape (2x2 Matrix)
steps.description: Competitive positioning on two axes: Scope of Responsibility x Domain Specificity. Helix alone occupies the system-level/ERP-specialized quadrant. Oracle/NetSuite Next is the primary threat, addressed with the platform-vs-accountability differentiation.

steps.title: Section 6: Traction Dashboard
steps.description: Production metrics from runtime inspection: 261 tickets, 123 deployed (47%), 71% monthly active rate, 16% MoM growth. Weak metrics (55% run success, 31% NS deployment, zero revenue) presented with honest context and improvement trajectories.

steps.title: Section 7: Risk Register
steps.description: 8 investor risks with severity, evidence, and mitigations. Top risks: Oracle building it (High), zero revenue (High), cost scaling (Medium-High). Pre-emptive responses to top 3 investor questions included.

steps.title: Section 8: Strategic Roadmap
steps.description: 3-phase roadmap: NetSuite Dominance (0-12mo), Multi-ERP Expansion (12-24mo), Platform Play (24-36mo). Architecture readiness for multi-ERP cited via native-phase.ts isolation.

steps.title: Section 9: Key Quotes & Positioning
steps.description: 10 verbatim Manifesto quotes with page attribution and suggested slide use. 8 slide headlines mapped to report sections. Website messaging recommendations for gethelix.ai.

steps.title: Appendix: Data Sources & Methodology
steps.description: Complete data source catalog (17 sources), methodology description, and full table of 12 runtime database queries with results -- providing full auditability for every claim in the report.
